title: Search feature subsets and finalize the best LDA fit in one call
summary: Ladder-beam subset search over feature columns using in-sample total log-likelihood of the LDA fit as the objective. Produces a top-per-k table, a refitted best-subset coefficient sheet, and a run-info sheet.
description: Loads a wide-format training CSV, builds an unconstrained pool over all feature columns, and runs a beam ladder that sweeps k from `k_min` to `k_max` (both directions from a central anchor). At each size the best-scoring subset is retained; each candidate is optionally improved with a local 1-swap. The best overall subset is refitted and written as a coefficient table. Emits a single started / finished event pair.
usage:
[["model-dfa::search-finalize"]]
input = "<wide.csv>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "dfa_search"
y_col = "y"
# optional:
k_min = 1
k_max = 8
beam_width = 25
step_top_n = 10

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: y_col
arguments.description: Name or 1-based index of the class-label column. Labels must be exactly 1..=K.

arguments.name: x_cols
arguments.description: Comma-separated specifier picking which columns to use as features. Default: all columns except y_col.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the input CSV has a header row.

arguments.name: k_max
arguments.description: Largest subset size to evaluate. Defaults to the number of eligible features (`p`).

arguments.name: beam_width
arguments.description: Beam width carried between ladder steps.

arguments.name: step_top_n
arguments.description: Top-N children retained per parent at each ladder step.

arguments.name: local_improve
arguments.description: Apply a local 1-swap refinement to each beam candidate.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of Rayon worker threads used to score candidate subsets in parallel at each ladder step. `1` (default) runs serially. Results are deterministic under a given `random_seed` regardless of thread count.

details: The objective is `sum_i log P(y_i | x_i)` under the LDA fit to the subset — the same total log-likelihood returned by `fit-finalize`. The search is a beam ladder: an anchor search at the midpoint of `[k_min, k_max]` seeds a descent toward `k_min` and an ascent toward `k_max`, both with a frontier of up to `beam_width` candidates. Each candidate expands by drop (down-step) or add (up-step); the best `step_top_n` children feed the next frontier. With `local_improve = true` each child is improved by a local 1-swap until no drop+add pair raises the objective.
value:
On success, `{run_dir}/ocinfo/{run_name}_dfa/` contains:

- `{run_name}_dfa_search_summary.csv` — one row per k with the best subset found (rank, k, total_llh, item indices/names, scenario_id).
- `{run_name}_dfa_search_summary.json` — same rows plus ladder diagnostics.
- `{run_name}_dfa_search_report.xlsx` — `top_subsets`, `best_coefficients`, and `run_info` sheets.

The primary xlsx is also copied to `{run_dir}/{run_name}_dfa_search.xlsx`.
notes: In-sample total log-likelihood will always favour larger k; compare `cv-search-finalize` (Phase D) for an unbiased selection metric.
Subsets whose induced pooled covariance is singular are silently assigned `-inf` and are pruned from the ladder.

see_also.description: Fit LDA on a fixed feature set.
Cross-validated LDA fit on a fixed feature set.

examples.title: Search with bounded k range
examples.body:
[["model-dfa::search-finalize"]]
input = "./wide.csv"
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "pilot_search_k25"
y_col = "y"
k_min = 2
k_max = 5
beam_width = 50
step_top_n = 20

fields.name: y_col
fields.description: Name or 1-based index of the class-label column.

fields.name: step_top_n
fields.description: Top-N children retained per parent at each ladder step.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Rayon worker threads used to score candidates in parallel. Default: 1.

artifacts.title: Feature-search report workbook
artifacts.description: The key output for feature selection using LDA. Shows the best variable subset at each size k, the coefficients for the best overall subset, and run metadata. Use this to decide how many variables to include and which ones matter most.

fields.name: top_subsets sheet
fields.description: Best subset at each k, ranked by total log-likelihood.

fields.name: best_coefficients sheet
fields.description: LDA coefficients for the best-overall subset.

artifacts.title: Search summary table
artifacts.description: One row per k with the best subset found. Use this for programmatic processing of the search results.

fields.name: k
fields.description: Number of features in the subset.

fields.name: total_llh
fields.description: Total log-likelihood of the LDA fit on this subset.

fields.name: items
fields.description: Feature names in the best subset at this k.
